description: The Winter Hill Gang was a loose confederation of American organized crime figures based in the Boston area, primarily active from 1961 to 2000. Founded by James 'Buddy' McLean and Howard 'Howie' Winter, it was predominantly Irish-American and involved in racketeering, drug trafficking, gambling, and weapons smuggling. The gang was the second most powerful criminal organization in New England after the Patriarca crime family. It was taken over by James 'Whitey' Bulger in 1979 and eventually dissolved.

country_links.evidence_quote: In September 1984, the Valhalla, a fishing boat, left Boston harbor loaded with weapons. The vessel was seized by two Irish Naval Service ships upon arriving in Ireland...
country_links.context: The Winter Hill Gang smuggled weapons to the Provisional Irish Republican Army (IRA) in Ireland.

country_links.evidence_quote: In December 1974, Callahan became the president and chief executive officer of World Jai Alai (WJA), a Florida-based sports betting enterprise which operated jai alai frontons in Miami, Tampa, Ocala, and Fort Pierce.
country_links.context: The Winter Hill Gang operated throughout the United States, including in Massachusetts, Florida, Oklahoma, and other states, engaging in illegal gambling, drug trafficking, and murder.
